path._doc: Selects the native fp8 MFMA fast path (AMD fnuz operands), the dequant-then-dot portable path, or auto routing. Accepted by the triton entry callable.

dot_bf16._doc: Portable-path only: cast dequantized operands to bf16 for the tl.dot (CDNA3 bf16 MFMA, fp32 accumulate). Faster in principle, ~bf16-accurate; forces the portable path.

perf.regime: Dense GEMM, fp8 block-scale. Default fp32-dot is exact but does not use the native fp8 MFMA path; on gfx942 the tuned-BLAS torch reference currently beats the naive Triton tiling (issue #38 honest negative result). The native fp8 MFMA path (path='mfma', fnuz operands) is the planned fast path. Perf is arch-gated and unmeasured without a GPU.

notes: Portable Triton (runs on NVIDIA and AMD); one program per (row-tile, col-tile), dequant in registers (operands never materialized in DRAM). Internal autotune tile space (BLOCK_M/BLOCK_N/BLOCK_K, num_warps, num_stages) lives inside the @triton.autotune kernels and is not exposed at the entry callable, so it is intentionally NOT declared in specialization_knobs (the harness would report it unapplied); it will be plumbed when a GPU measurement lands. wave_size is not load-bearing at this metadata tier (arch-specific tile math is encoded in the JIT kernels). A100 (sm80) is missing_arch: no fp8 units, so the Triton fp8 load does not compile (CompilationError at the fp8 tl.load) — expected, not a bug (issue #86).
